notes: Training jobs are read-only with respect to canonical datasets and managed feature datasets.
Python targets must define def compute_target(step): ...
Execution targets must define def execute_trade(ctx): ... or omit code to use ctx.default_execute(direction).
Execution targets in label_mode='custom_code' must define def compute_label(ctx, outcome): ... to project one forward-looking scalar label from the normalized trade outcome.
Execution targets may add target_transform when you want a derived label such as net_pnl > 0 without inventing a python_target.
Only deployable declarative inputs may be used from step.model_predict(...) inside feature runtime.

target_modes.description: Align a raw or feature source column to the anchor timeframe and use it directly as target.
Define def compute_target(step): ... and compute one scalar target per anchor row from the current and historical context only.

Define def execute_trade(ctx): ... or use ctx.default_execute(direction) to label each anchor row from simulated trade outcomes, then either project one builtin outcome field or compute a custom scalar label.

decision_rules.when: You want labels derived from future trade execution, profitability, TP/SL hits, duration, exit reason, or PnL.
decision_rules.because: python_target only receives current and past context; it cannot look into future candles.

decision_rules.when: You want a custom binary or scalar label computed from forward-looking execution context and the normalized trade outcome.
decision_rules.because: This keeps future-aware logic in the execution runtime while letting compute_label(ctx, outcome) emit the final scalar training label.

decision_rules.when: You want labels derived only from current and historical context available at the anchor candle.
decision_rules.because: compute_target(step) is evaluated row-by-row on the current and past context only.

decision_rules.when: You already have the aligned target as a raw or feature column.
decision_rules.because: column_target is the simplest and most reproducible target mode for precomputed labels.

dataset_spec_python_target.name: BTC 1h current-context labels
dataset_spec_python_target.description: Python target example that only uses current and historical context.

target.code:
def compute_target(step):
    return 'bull' if step.current('close') > step.current('open') else 'bear'

dataset_spec_execution_target_profit_cla.name: BTC 1h profit labels
dataset_spec_execution_target_profit_cla.description: Execution target example for long-profit classification.

dataset_spec_execution_target_profit_reg.name: BTC 1h net-pnl labels
dataset_spec_execution_target_profit_reg.description: Execution target example for regression on trade net_pnl.

dataset_spec_execution_target_custom_bin.name: BTC 1h custom forward label
dataset_spec_execution_target_custom_bin.description: Execution target example for custom forward-looking binary labels.

common_failures.remediation: Inspect models_train(action='status', include=['logs','result','artifacts']) before retrying with new params or a revised dataset.
Inspect deployability_report in models_dataset(view='detail') or models_registry(action='inspect', view='version_detail') and remove analysis_input_code or unsupported feature timeframes.

common_failures.when: python_target is used for future trade profitability or other execution-derived labels
common_failures.remediation: Switch to execution_target and use ctx.default_execute(direction) or a custom execute_trade(ctx) implementation.

common_failures.when: you need a custom binary or scalar label from forward-looking execution context
common_failures.remediation: Use execution_target(label_mode='custom_code') and define compute_label(ctx, outcome) instead of forcing the logic into python_target or target_transform.

common_failures.when: input_fields use source_kind='builtin', timeframe=null, or offset instead of lag
common_failures.remediation: Use source_kind 'ohlcv' or 'feature', always supply an explicit timeframe string, and use lag instead of offset.

predictive_cutoff_policy.warning: Predictive strategy-usable datasets and training runs cannot extend past this cutoff.

next_recommended_actions: Use models_dataset(action='preview', spec={...}) to validate one dataset spec against this contract.
Use models_dataset(action='materialize', spec={...}) only after the preview looks correct.
data.summary: Loaded the training dataset authoring contract.

predictive_training_cutoff.warning: spec.end_at must not exceed max_allowed_end_at for predictive_signal strategy_usage_class.
